title: Sensitive history — domestic violence screening
candidateInstructions: You are an FY2 GP. Mrs Helen Fletcher, a 34-year-old woman, has attended her third appointment in 2 months with soft tissue injuries. Today she has a bruised forearm and says she fell down stairs. Previous attendances were for a black eye (walked into a door) and rib bruising (car accident). Please take a sensitive history.
examinerNotes: Domestic violence station. Candidate must: use DASH screening, ask directly about abuse after building rapport, not interrogate in front of partner (partner is in waiting room), provide safety information, involve MARAC/IDVA if high risk. Must not minimise or dismiss. Critical error: having the partner present during questioning.

patient.name: Helen Fletcher
patient.presentingComplaint: Bruised forearm — says she fell down stairs
patient.reasonForAttendance: I fell down the stairs this morning. My arm really hurts.

socialHistory.livingArrangement: Lives with husband Mark (39) and 2 children aged 4 and 7

systemsReview.dermatological: Multiple bruises at various healing stages on arms

examination.general: Anxious woman, avoids eye contact, speaks quietly, flinches when examined
examination.musculoskeletal: Bruising right forearm. Multiple bruises at various stages of healing on upper arms. X-ray no fracture.

patient.emotionalState: Frightened, defensive initially. Will deny abuse if asked bluntly without rapport. If asked gently in a safe space will gradually open up. Will cry when discussing children. Is considering leaving but terrified.
patient.communicationStyle: Quiet, evasive initially. If made to feel safe and not judged will gradually confide. Watches the door nervously. Will admit to abuse when directly but gently asked after trust established.
hiddenConcerns: Mark hits me and the children sometimes
I'm scared to leave — he threatened to take the children
I don't want my kids taken away from me
I feel like it's my fault
I've thought about leaving but I have nowhere to go
doNotVolunteer: Abuse — will deny initially with standard story. Will open up only if doctor creates private, non-judgmental space
Children witnessing violence — only if directly asked about children's safety
Thoughts of leaving — only if trust is established

primaryDiagnosis: Domestic Violence (intimate partner violence) — high-risk pattern

dataGathering.criterion: Ensures patient is seen alone — without partner present
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Confirms partner is NOT in room, creates safe space

dataGathering.criterion: Builds rapport before asking directly about abuse
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Spends time building trust before direct questioning

dataGathering.criterion: Asks directly about domestic abuse in a non-judgmental way
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: 'Sometimes when people have injuries like this, it can be because someone has hurt them at home — is that something that might be happening to you?'
Asks if children are safe, whether they witness or experience violence

clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: National Domestic Violence Hotline, IDVA (Independent Domestic Violence Advisor) referral
Explains will document injuries accurately — may be needed as evidence

interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Never blames patient, validates her feelings, does not minimise

interpersonalSkills.criterion: Respects patient's autonomy — she decides when/if to act
interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Does not pressure patient to leave immediately

references.source: NICE PH50: Domestic violence and abuse — multi-agency response

learningObjectives: Identify pattern of injuries consistent with intimate partner violence
Ask directly about domestic abuse sensitively
Provide appropriate safety information and referrals
